No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to poverty eradication or economic support for the vulnerable.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3). It aims to impart specialized knowledge and skills (Target 4.4) and cultivate humanistic literacy and cultural understanding (Target 4.7).
evidence: 専門的な知識・技能の習得を目指します (acquire specialized knowledge and skills)
培養學生具備人文素養，知曉日本社會與歷史文化 (cultivate humanistic literacy, understanding of Japanese society and history/culture)

Gender Equality.reason: The course explicitly covers 'gender and language' (ジェンダーとことば), directly relating to understanding and addressing gender discrimination (Target 5.1, 5.c).
evidence: ジェンダーとことば (gender and language)

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course aims to develop language abilities and professional competitiveness required for work, directly supporting skills for productive employment (Target 8.5).
evidence: 工作所需之語言能力，以及專業競爭力 (language ability... required for... work, as well as professional competitiveness)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to industrialization, infrastructure development, or technological innovation in these sectors.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course analyzes 'language and society,' including 'gender and language' and 'dialects,' which directly addresses social inclusion and potential inequalities (Target 10.2, 10.3).
evidence: ことばと社会 (language and society)
ジェンダーとことば (gender and language)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to urban development, housing, transport, or cultural heritage protection.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to sustainable consumption, waste reduction, or resource management.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to climate change, adaptation, or mitigation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: By analyzing the 'relationship between language and society,' including gender, the course indirectly supports the promotion of non-discriminatory policies (Target 16.b).
evidence: ことばと社会の関係性 (the relationship between language and society)
ジェンダーとことば (gender and language)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course aims to 'cultivate an international perspective' and 'international mobility,' which are foundational for building global partnerships and knowledge sharing (Target 17.16).
evidence: 培養學生具備國際觀、國際移動力 (cultivate an international perspective, international mobility)
